title: From milking cows to making millions: How Syracuse’s property brothers built a real estate empire
summary: Syracuse-based IronHorn Enterprises, founded by brothers Colin and Greg Cleghorn, has built a $275M real estate portfolio focused on industrial properties and e-commerce warehousing across 17 states. The company's growth demonstrates regional entrepreneurial success and has generated local employment; recent acquisitions include the former Rite Aid distribution center (Clay) and warehouse leases to regional businesses like Beak & Skiff Apple Orchards.

political_signal.description: Article highlights private-sector economic development success and job creation in Syracuse region, aligning with CIO/municipal economic development priorities. Industrial property growth driven by e-commerce logistics represents infrastructure modernization trend.
